value.setup: Cushing draws five straight weeks toward tank-bottom tightness.
value.rationale: Cushing fell to 18.96 mb, down 1.08 this week and from 23.02 mb on May 22, a relentless 4 mb decline in a month. Such low hub stocks tighten prompt WTI deliverability and support backwardation. Watch for any Cushing rebuild or import surge as the catalyst to fade the steepening.

value.setup: Large headline crude draw with rising exports and high runs.
value.rationale: US crude stocks drew 6.09 mb to 412.134 mb while exports rose to 4.93 mbd and runs held at 17.11 mbd with 96.1% utilization. PADD3 crude drew a heavy 3.98 mb, confirming Gulf tightness. Key risk is the slight dip in refiner inputs (-0.08 mbd) signaling demand softening.

value.setup: Distillate builds but PADD1 draws sharply as gasoline overhangs.
value.rationale: Distillate built 3.06 mb nationally yet PADD1 drew 2.12 mb amid a steep 10.6-point utilization collapse to 76.5%, tightening East Coast diesel supply. Meanwhile gasoline built 2.06 mb with 4-week demand slipping to 8.83 from 8.90 mbd. Watch PADD1 refinery restarts as the reversal catalyst.

value.setup: PADD1 gasoline build coincides with softening national demand.
value.rationale: PADD1 gasoline stocks rose 1.21 mb and national gasoline built 2.06 mb to 216.299 mb, while 4-week gasoline demand eased to 8.83 mbd from 8.90. Collapsing PADD1 utilization (-10.6 to 76.5%) usually supports basis, but the inventory overhang and weak demand argue for near-term pressure. Catalyst risk is a demand rebound into peak summer driving season.
